title: Oil India CNC Programmer cum Operator Recruitment 2026 - Walkin
short_info: Oil India (OIL) CNC Programmer cum Operator Notification 2026 has been released on 08 May 2026 on the official website of Oil India . A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the OIL CNC Programmer cum Operator Recruitment 2026. Candidates aged between 18 to 40 years who have Diploma in Mechanical/Production/Electrical/Electronics & Tele-Communication along with Certificate Course in Assistant Operator-CNC Turning and minimum 2 years post-qualification experience on CNC Machines are eligible to apply for these CNC Programmer cum Operator vacancies. The walk-in registration and personal

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 40 years Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.details: CNC Programmer cum Operator: Must have passed Diploma in Mechanical/Production/Electrical/Electronics & Tele-Communication or equivalent examination of minimum 02 (two) years duration from a Government Recognized Institute/Board. Must possess Certificate Course in Assistant Operator- CNC Turning or equivalent certificate course of minimum 06 (six) months duration or 600 hours duration from a recognized institute. Must have minimum 02 (Two) years post-qualification work experience on programming, operating and working in different types of CNC Machines including hands-on operation of CNC Lathe in any Govt./PSU/Reputed Private Organization.

salary_details.details: Fixed emolument: ₹ 24,960.00 per month based on attendance including paid leave, holiday, if any. Variable emolument: ₹ 960.00 per day for each working day.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for Oil India Limited Recruitment?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator post is scheduled for 01-06-2026. Candidates should ensure they reach the venue on this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the minimum and maximum age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit is 18 years, and the maximum age limit is 40 years for all posts. Age relaxation will be applicable as per government norms.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Diploma in Mechanical/Production/Electrical/Electronics & Tele-Communication, a Certificate Course in Assistant Operator-CNC Turning, and a minimum of 2 years of post-qualification experience.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are available for the Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator position?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy available for the Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator post in Oil India Limited.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for this contractual role?
faq.answer: The salary offered is a fixed emolument of ₹ 24,960.00 per month, plus a variable emolument of ₹ 960.00 per day for each working day.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) in the official notification for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator post?
faq.answer: The selection process will be based on a Walk-in for Personal Assessment(s). Candidates need to attend the interview with all required documents.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted in Duliajan, Assam. The exact venue details will be available in the official notification.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should I carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, self-attested photocopies of educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and passport-size photographs.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent job?
faq.answer: No, this is a contractual job. The position is for a Contractual CNC Programmer cum Operator, and the employment terms will be as per the contract.
